titleStmt.title: The British Prime Minister ( (Churchill) ) to President Roosevelt

div.head:
The British Prime Minister ( Churchill ) to President Roosevelt
Paraphrase of telegram handed to the Secretary of State by the British Ambassador on December 26.
div.p:
858. AnthonyAnthony Eden, British Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs. and I are going out to see what we can do to square
this Greek entanglement. The basis of action:— The King does not go back until a plebiscite in his favour has been taken. For the rest, we cannot abandon those who have taken up arms in our cause, and must of necessity [? fight it out with them].Brackets appear in the file copy; the clause as printed in Winston S. Churchill, Triumph and Tragedy, p. 315, reads “and must if necessary fight it out with them”.

It must always be understood that we seek nothing from Greece, in territory or advantage. We have given much and will give more if it is in our power. I count on you to help us in this time of unusual difficulty. In particular I should like you to tell your Ambassador in Athens to make contact with us and to help all he can in accordance with above principles.
